danger_signals.visual_form: Life bar flashing red at 25% or below
danger_signals.meaning: Player character is in danger of defeat; Desperation Move (Fury) becomes available as high-risk/high-reward option

danger_signals.visual_form: Non-playable character designs with distinct visual styling (Billy Kane, Wolfgang Krauser, Laurence Blood, Axel Hawk)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; progression milestone requiring defeat of stronger opponents

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental threats visible in alternate fighting plane (electrified wires, bull stampede)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; knocking opponent to hazard plane causes extra damage; plane-switching becomes strategically critical

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual transition showing opponent repositioned to background fighting plane
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; opponent exposed to plane-specific hazards or distant positioning disadvantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Character blocking stance with visual feedback
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive success enables Evasion Attack counterattack opportunity; momentary safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Character movement away from opponent via rapid backward lever input
safety_signals.meaning: Spatial safety; distance management to reset engagement or avoid incoming attacks

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character on foreground plane while opponent on background plane
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic positioning safety; player closer to camera has engagement advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Martial discipline visual markers (gi, training clothes, fighting stance types)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: South Town and international tournament venues; progression through global locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wolfgang Krauser as German nobleman sponsor; formal character designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier visual distinction from playable fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament sponsorship structure; mysterious nobleman orchestrating global King of Fighters competition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Billy Kane as bojutsu master seeking revenge for Geese's death
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss character with distinct fighting style and visual presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Continuation of first game's antagonistic narrative; revenge motivation as character driver

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character nationality as core identity (Terry/Andy=American, Joe=Japanese, Kim=Korean, Cheng=Hong Kong, Mai=Japanese ninja, Big Bear=Australian, Jubei=Japanese, Billy=UK, Laurence=Spanish bullfighter, Krauser=German)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Global tournament structure; world-spanning battle locations matching character origins

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Geese death; power vacuum in martial arts world
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New mysterious nobleman sponsor replaces previous antagonistic structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Billy Kane and other revenge-seeking characters actively hunting tournament winner; tournament expanded globally rather than localized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent roster progression: 8 playable characters → 4 boss characters; visual complexity increases with boss designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss characters display non-playable status; more aggressive AI patterns; higher damage output

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life gauge flashing red at 25% threshold
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Desperation Move becomes available; high-risk strategic pivot point; visual urgency cues

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background plane contains lethal environmental elements (electrified wires, bull stampede)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot freely change planes; must strategically knock opponent to hazard plane for extra damage; hazard becomes tactical weapon

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character design as national/cultural identity marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Mai Shiranui's kunoichi design, Kim Kaphwan's taekwondo gi, Cheng Sinzan's Shifu appearance, Laurence Blood's matador aesthetic visually establish character origin and fighting discipline without explicit exposition
Billy Kane, Wolfgang Krauser, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood visually differ from playable character roster, signaling elevated threat and narrative importance
Stage-specific background hazards (bull stampede in matador-associated stage) reflect character origin stories and cultural contexts; environment becomes extension of character narrative

Progression from 8 playable fighters to 4 boss encounters mirrors story escalation; mysterious nobleman systematically defeating first game's champions establishes antagonistic force without direct confrontation until endgame
Elderly Jubei Yamada's visual design signals veteran/mentor archetype; Big Bear's wrestler appearance distinct from martial artists; Axel Hawk's boxer physique visually separates fighting discipline

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Desperation Move becomes available; visual life gauge flashes red; strategic shift toward high-risk offensive maneuvers

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Knocked to background plane with hazards present
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Takes extra damage from environmental hazards; positioning disadvantage; vulnerability window for plane-specific attacks (Low/High Plane Move Attacks)

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss characters (Billy Kane, Wolfgang Krauser, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Non-playable status; enhanced AI aggression; higher damage output; narrative significance as antagonistic forces or revenge-seekers
Successful block opens Evasion Attack counterattack window; temporary vulnerability state despite defensive posture
Systematically defeats previous tournament champions; searches for Geese killer; remains hidden/non-confrontational until endgame progression

notes: Fatal Fury 2 uses visual character design and national identity as primary world-state communicator. The game emphasizes global faction differentiation through character origin, fighting discipline visual markers (gi types, weapon designs, body type), and cultural aesthetic choices. Danger is communicated through boss-tier visual distinction, critical health threshold flashing, and environmental hazard visibility. The narrative world-state (post-Geese power vacuum, mysterious sponsor searching for tournament winner) is conveyed through character roster progression and tournament structure rather than explicit exposition. The two-plane fighting system creates additional danger/safety visual language through spatial positioning and hazard visibility. T2 tier data: specific visual color values, exact animation frame counts, or precise difficulty metrics not available from source material.
